description_en: Old Painting of Fudō Myōō is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, the attendants Kongara and Seitaka Dōji are depicted at its sides. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Oshibo 1469, Kamisato.

name_en: Old Bronze Standing Statue of Shō-Kannon
description_en: Old Bronze Standing Statue of Shō-Kannon is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is said to be the personal guardian icon of Shinra Saburō Yoshimitsu and a relic of Yōun-ji. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

Statue of Shaka Nyorai is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is said to be a relic of Yōun'in. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

Standing Statue of Shō-Kannon is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a gilt joined-wood statue with inlaid crystal eyes, held by temple tradition to be the work of the priest Eshin Sōzu. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 67, Kamisato.

Seated Statue of Amida Nyorai is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a gilt statue with inlaid crystal eyes, recorded as the largest Buddhist statue in the town. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Ōmidō 1151, Kamisato.

Gilt-Bronze Statue of Shaka Nyorai is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it bears an inscription dated the 17th day of the 9th month of Hōreki 13 (given as 1736) by the caster Tagawa Minbu of Kanda, Edo. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 3316-1, Kamisato.

Seated Statue of Yakushi is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it bears an ink inscription dated Keichō 10 (1605). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Tsutsumi 474, Kamisato.

Shōgun Jizō (Victory Jizō) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a wooden statue with lacquer gilding, vermilion coloring, and inlaid crystal eyes. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Jinbohara-machi 263, Kamisato.

Bronze Bell is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it was made by the Sano metal caster Inoue Genpō on the 23rd day of the 10th month of Genroku 8 (1695). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

name_en: Sword Donated by Lord Sanjō Sanetomi
description_en: Sword Donated by Lord Sanjō Sanetomi is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is signed 'Nagahiro of Suō Province' and is accompanied by a note by Takahashi Deishū. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

name_en: Certificate of Full Payment Issued by Tokugawa Ieyasu
description_en: Certificate of Full Payment Issued by Tokugawa Ieyasu is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is dated Keichō 11 (1606) and addressed to the magistrate Hasegawa Nagatsugu. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on October 29, 1987. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara 1369, Kamisato.

name_en: Yakushi Sutra Transcription Brushed by Zen Master Kōsen
description_en: Yakushi Sutra Transcription Brushed by Zen Master Kōsen is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a transcription of the Yakushi Nyorai sutra in gold ink on a dark blue ground. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Nagahama 1774, Kamisato.

Medieval Documents of Yōun-ji Temple is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, they are seven medieval documents connected with the Takeda family. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 27, 1974. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

Land Register of Bunroku 4 is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it covers Mayuzumi village within Kanakubo, Hachigata district, Kami county, Bushū Province, dated Bunroku 4 (1595). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Mayuzumi 86, Kamisato.

Written Authorization by Archbishop Tenkai is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a Tōeizan direct-branch temple certificate dated the 28th day of the 3rd month of Kan'ei 19 (1642). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Mayuzumi, Kamisato.

Parent-and-Child Jizō is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is an incised stone stupa tablet bearing images of Jizō on both faces, said to be the grave of Teshigawara Naoshige and his child. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara 1864, Kamisato.

Head of a Haniwa Figure is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is said to have been excavated at the confluence of the Kanna and Karasu rivers. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1974. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 67, Kamisato.

Cloth-Patterned Roof Tiles is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, they are tiles excavated from the Gomyō temple ruins: three types of flat eave-tiles and three types of round eave-tiles. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Gomyō 761, Kamisato.

Stone Rod (Sekibō) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is the sacred object of Ishi Shrine. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Jinbohara-machi 1384, Kamisato.

Stone Rod (Sekibō) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is said to have been enshrined as a sacred object. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 1950, Kamisato.

Sengen'yama Tumulus is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it has a horizontal stone chamber built of hornblende andesite. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Jinbohara-machi 15, Kamisato.

Stone Slab Stupa is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is inscribed with the date Kōchō 4 (1264). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 594-1, Kamisato.

name_en: Haniwa Group Excavated from Teraura Tumulus No. 1
description_en: Haniwa Group Excavated from Teraura Tumulus No. 1 is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a group of five haniwa excavated from Teraura Tumulus No. 1 in Ōaza Nagahama, comprising four male and female human figures and one horse figure. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on December 22, 2021. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 67, Kamisato.

See-Through Stone Lantern is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it was erected in Bunka 12 (1815). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara 1864, Kamisato.

Stone Pillar Lantern (Sekidō) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a six-sided Jizō stone pillar inscribed with the date Eishō 6 (1509). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara 1864, Kamisato.

Akabane Swords is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, they are six swords, spears and glaives confiscated during the war. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on September 15, 2001. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi 67, Kamisato.

Oshibo Kagura (Sacred Dance) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a kagura sacred dance handed down at Ikegami Shrine. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Oshibo, Kamisato.

Mayuzumi Ondo (Folk Song and Dance) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is an ondo performed with barrel drum, hand drum, bell and flute, together with a lead singer and dancers. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Mayuzumi, Kamisato.

Lion Dance of Suwa Shrine is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, its lion-head mask is inscribed with the date Jōkyō 2 (1685). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Sanchō, Kamisato.

Lion Dance of Nyū Shrine is classified as a tangible cultural property. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Teshigawara, Kamisato.

Lion Dance of Kanakubo Shrine is classified as a tangible cultural property. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo, Kamisato.

Lion Dance of Shichihongi Shrine is classified as a tangible cultural property. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on November 3, 1976. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi, Kamisato.

Azuma Ondo (Folk Song and Dance) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is an ondo originating in the Nagahata district. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: the Nagahata district, Kamisato.

Kanbu Kanna River Drums is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a creative Japanese drum group active since 1991 (Heisei 3), performing within and beyond the town, including overseas. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on December 22, 2021. Recorded location: Ōaza Shichihongi, Kamisato.

Grave of Yōun'in is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is the grave of Yōun'in, a consort of Takeda Shingen. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.

Grave of Tatewaki-senjō Yoshikata is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is the grave of Minamoto no Yoshikata. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Tatewaki 309-2, Kamisato.

Five-Ring Stupa (Gorintō) is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is a pre-death memorial for the priest Enshū, dated Tenbun 15 (1546). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Fujikido 29-1, Kamisato.

name_en: Old Battlefield of the Kanna River
description_en: Old Battlefield of the Kanna River is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is the site of the Battle of the Kanna River, fought in Tenshō 10 (1582). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Bishato, Kamisato.

Site of Kanakubo Manor is classified as a tangible cultural property. According to the record, it is said to have been built by Kaji Ietoshi during the Jishō era (1177-1180). It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 1570 and elsewhere, Kamisato.

Memorial Shrine for Hata Tokiyoshi is classified as a tangible cultural property. It holds town-designated status under Kamisato and was designated on February 22, 1962. Recorded location: Ōaza Kanakubo 701, Kamisato.
